title: G1: Rationally generating family of 4k-manifolds without cobordism theory

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Central gap.** The standard proof reduces the signature theorem to checking two ring homomorphisms ($\tau$ and $\langle L,[\cdot]\rangle$) agree on generators of $\Omega^{SO}_*\otimes\mathbb{Q}=\mathbb{Q}[\,[\mathbb{CP}^2],[\mathbb{CP}^4],\dots]$ (Thom). The problem **explicitly bans cobordism theory / Pontryagin–Thom**, so this generation statement is not available off the shelf.

**What must be proved.** In each dimension $4k$, exhibit an explicit finite family of closed oriented manifolds $M^{(1)},\dots,M^{(N)}$ (e.g. products $\mathbb{CP}^{2i_1}\times\cdots\times\mathbb{CP}^{2i_r}$ and Milnor hypersurfaces $H_{i,j}\subset\mathbb{CP}^i\times\mathbb{CP}^j$) whose **Pontryagin-number vectors are linearly independent** (span the space of Pontryagin numbers in degree $4k$, dimension $=p(k)$ = number of partitions of $k$), and on which both $\tau$ and $\langle L,[\cdot]\rangle$ can be computed in closed form. Then conclude the two linear functionals on Pontryagin-number space coincide.

**Risk to audit:** any argument that 'these manifolds generate rationally' must not covertly re-invoke $\Omega^{SO}_*\otimes\mathbb{Q}$; the spanning must be a direct (e.g. determinant/Vandermonde-type) computation. This is the hardest gap and the reason 'elementary' is a real constraint.
